verses.1: A wise son accepts his father’s discipline, But a scoffer does not listen to rebuke.
verses.2: From the fruit of a person’s mouth he enjoys good, But the desire of the treacherous is violence.
verses.3: One who guards his mouth protects his life; One who opens wide his lips comes to ruin.
verses.4: The soul of the lazy one craves and gets nothing, But the soul of the diligent is made prosperous.
verses.5: A righteous person hates a false statement, But a wicked person acts disgustingly and shamefully.
verses.6: Righteousness guards the one whose way is blameless, But wickedness brings the sinner to ruin.
verses.7: There is one who pretends to be rich but has nothing; Another pretends to be poor, but has great wealth.
verses.8: The ransom of a person’s life is his wealth, But the poor hears no rebuke.
verses.9: The light of the righteous rejoices, But the lamp of the wicked goes out.
verses.10: Through overconfidence comes nothing but strife, But wisdom is with those who receive counsel.
verses.11: Wealth obtained from nothing dwindles, But one who gathers by labor increases it.
verses.12: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, But desire fulfilled is a tree of life.
verses.13: One who despises the word will do badly, But one who fears the commandment will be rewarded.
verses.14: The teaching of the wise is a fountain of life, To turn aside from the snares of death.
verses.15: Good understanding produces favor, But the way of the treacherous is their own disaster.
verses.16: Every prudent person acts with knowledge, But a fool displays foolishness.
verses.17: A wicked messenger falls into adversity, But a faithful messenger brings healing.
verses.18: Poverty and shame will come to one who neglects discipline, But one who complies with rebuke will be honored.
verses.19: Desire realized is sweet to the soul, But it is an abomination to fools to turn away from evil.
verses.20: One who walks with wise people will be wise, But a companion of fools will suffer harm.
verses.21: Adversity pursues sinners, But the righteous will be rewarded with prosperity.
verses.22: A good person leaves an inheritance to his grandchildren, And the wealth of a sinner is stored up for the righteous.
verses.23: Abundant food is in the uncultivated ground of the poor, But it is swept away by injustice.
verses.24: He who withholds his rod hates his son, But he who loves him disciplines him diligently.
verses.25: The righteous has enough to satisfy his appetite, But the stomach of the wicked is in need.
